science.title: This donut-shaped discovery just shattered a 150-year math rule
science.summary: A 150-year-old rule in geometry has been proven wrong. Mathematicians found two different doughnut-shaped surfaces that look identical when measured locally but are actually different overall. For decades, researchers suspected this might be possible but couldn’t prove it—until now. The breakthrough reshapes how mathem

science.title: Specially designed material combines light and electricity to remove PFAS from water without harmful byproducts
science.summary: Researchers at Clarkson University have reported a breakthrough in tackling per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS), a group of widely used "forever chemicals" that are difficult to remove from water and have raised growing environmental and public health concerns. science.title: Breakthrough Crystal Lets Scientists “Write” Nanoscale Patterns With Light
science.summary: A team of scientists has uncovered a crystal that can be reshaped and programmed using ordinary light, opening a new path for building optical technology. Researchers at the XPANCEO Emerging Technologies Research Center, working alongside Nobel Laureate Prof. Konstantin Novoselov (University of Manchester and the Natio

science.title: Scientists just captured a mysterious quantum “dance” inside superconductors
science.summary: In a breakthrough experiment, scientists directly imaged how particles pair up in a system that mimics superconductors. Instead of behaving independently, the pairs moved in a synchronized, dance-like pattern—something never predicted before. This suggests a major gap in the classic theory of superconductivity.

science.title: Mezcal worm in a bottle DNA test reveals a surprise
science.summary: The famous mezcal “worm” has long puzzled scientists, but DNA testing has finally cracked the case. Researchers found that all sampled larvae were actually agave redworm moth caterpillars—not a mix of species as once believed. medicine.title: Simple “gut reset” may stop weight gain after Ozempic or Wegovy

medicine.title: Hidden virus in your gut may be linked to colon cancer
medicine.summary: A newly discovered virus hiding inside a common gut bacterium could help explain one of medicine’s long-standing mysteries: why a microbe found in both healthy people and cancer patients is linked to colorectal cancer. medicine.title: Two common drugs may reverse fatty liver disease, study finds
medicine.summary: Scientists have discovered that combining two existing drugs can dramatically reduce liver fat linked to a common and often silent disease. astronomy.title: The Planet Haul That Changes Everything.
astronomy.summary: NASA's planet hunting telescope has been busy. A new study has just sifted through the light of over 83 million stars and emerged with more than 11,000 potential worlds, including a confirmed giant planet orbiting a distant star. astronomy.title: JWST Hunts for an 'Earth-Moon' Twin in a Habitable Zone, But the Star Has Other Plans
astronomy.summary: The Moon has played a huge role in the development of Earth. It stabilizes the planet, tempered dramatic climate swings, and possibly even provided the tidal heating that might have led to the first life forms. So it’s natural we would want to find a similar Earth/Luna system somewhere else in the cosmos. But astronome

astronomy.title: Two Worlds Where the Sun Never Moves
astronomy.summary: One side is scorched to over 200 degrees, while the other is plunged into a darkness so cold it falls below minus 200. Welcome to TRAPPIST-1b and 1c, two rocky worlds that have just revealed the first ever climate maps of Earth sized planets beyond our Solar System. astronomy.title: James Webb Space Telescope peers into a dying star surrounded by mysterious buckyballs: 'The structures we're seeing now are breathtaking'
